```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  mu <- sample(seq(-3,4,0.5),1); ex2 <- mu^2+sample(seq(1,12,0.5),1)
  ans <- ex2-mu^2; vals <- round(c(ans,ex2+mu^2,sqrt(ex2),ex2-mu,mu^2),3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $E(X)=`r mu`$ e $E(X^2)=`r ex2`$, qual é $\operatorname{Var}(X)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
$$\operatorname{Var}(X)=E(X^2)-[E(X)]^2
=`r ex2`-(`r mu`)^2=`r sprintf("%.3f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S9Q1_identidade_variancia
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
